title: Aglaia pallens
taxonomy: Aglaia pallens is a species of flowering plant in the family Meliaceae. It belongs to the genus Aglaia, which is a group of tropical trees and shrubs native to Asia and the Pacific Islands. The Meliaceae family is a group of trees and shrubs that are commonly known as the mahogany family. Aglaia pallens is native to Australia and New Guinea, and it is found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is classified as a flowering plant, with small white flowers that are clustered together in panicles.
uses: Aglaia pallens, a plant in the Meliaceae family, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The bark and roots of the plant are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. In traditional medicine, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help in reducing swelling and fighting infections. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. However, the effectiveness and safety of these uses should be verified through scientific studies.

intro: The Aglaia pallens is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the velvet tree or the velvet meliaceous tree. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with various studies indicating its potential in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Bioactive constituents of Aglaia pallens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and limonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
